item.title: U.S. uses hundreds of Tomahawk missiles on Iran, alarming some at Pentagon
item.description: More than 850 Tomahawks have been fired in just four weeks, people familiar with the matter said, alarming some Pentagon officials because the weapon’s supply is limited.

item.title: Cuba looks to Vatican for help as U.S. pressure plunges island into crisis
item.description: Cuban officials have petitioned Pope Leo XIV to help persuade the Trump administration to ease its oil embargo, which is causing crippling fuel shortages and blackouts.

item.title: $500 tee times, private yachts: Bachelor(ette) trips are out of control
item.description: Bachelor and bachelorette trips have become increasingly elaborate and costly, with social media and influencer culture driving the trend.

item.title: Far fewer immigrants are moving to big cities in U.S., data shows
item.description: New York, Los Angeles and Chicago are among the metro areas seeing steep declines in net immigration amid the Trump administration’s crackdown.

item.title: Israel says it killed Iran’s top naval commander in bid to reopen Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Israel said Alireza Tangsiri, commander of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps’ navy, was responsible for blocking the Strait of Hormuz. Iranian authorities did not immediately comment.

item.title: Pentagon considers diverting Ukraine military aid to the Middle East
item.description: A shift would highlight the growing trade-offs required for the U.S. to sustain its war with Iran as the conflict depletes the military’s critical munitions.

item.title: Gulf countries want Trump to end Iran war — but not yet, officials say
item.description: The Saudis and Emiratis fear a deal that leaves the region less stable, and they have indicated support for an escalated campaign to force concessions from Tehran.

item.title: China bars executives at Meta-owned AI company from leaving country
item.description: Manus’s CEO and chief scientist are facing scrutiny from Beijing over the company’s $2 billion sale to Meta.

item.title: Iraq accuses U.S. of killing 7 soldiers in clinic strike
item.description: As Iraq claimed “heinous aggression,” the U.S. denied it targeted a clinic. The incident threatens to sour relations between the countries amid the Iran war.

item.title: Russia hits Ukrainian cities, intensifying bombing as U.S. focuses on Iran
item.description: Ukraine said eight people were killed as Russia launched a bombardment that included its largest single-day drone assault of the war.

item.title: Pakistan’s effort to mediate U.S.-Iran talks appears to gather steam
item.description: Iran praised Pakistan’s “good intentions” but did not say how it had responded. A Pakistani analyst described “significant and rapid progress” in indirect talks.

item.title: Iran’s missiles pierce Israel’s defenses, raising doubts about interceptors
item.description: Concern that Iran was amassing missiles to overwhelm defenses was a key factor in the push for war, officials said, and recent strikes laid bare Israel’s vulnerability.

item.title: Army paratroopers ordered to Middle East as U.S. weighs next Iran move
item.description: The Pentagon on Tuesday ordered a couple thousand paratroopers from the 82nd Airborne Division to deploy to the Middle East.

item.title: Audio from cockpit recorder reveals error before deadly LaGuardia crash
item.description: LaGuardia’s air traffic control tower failed to recognize it had granted permission for a plane and an emergency vehicle to use the same runway, officials said.

item.title: Justices seem inclined to revive Trump policy blocking migrants at border
item.description: The Supreme Court weighed a policy that would allow agents at U.S. borders to block migrants from entering the country to seek asylum.

item.title: Rare Middle East storm could bring floods, damaging winds and tornadoes
item.description: Parts of the Arabian Peninsula and Persian Gulf could be hit by strong thunderstorms later this week. Major highways and airports in the region could be inundated.

item.title: Trump says peace talks progressing as Iran officials deny negotiations
item.description: Egypt, Pakistan and Turkey have taken the lead in efforts to broker a peace deal between the United States and Iran, though conversations have been indirect thus far.

item.title: Israel and Iran exchange strikes as Trump says U.S. is negotiating end to war
item.description: Israel and three Gulf states reported Iranian attacks a day after President Donald Trump said the U.S. would pause strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure during talks.

item.title: He was Rubio’s friend and roommate. Now Rubio will testify against him.
item.description: Former congressman David Rivera, accused of secretly lobbying for Nicolás Maduro’s government in Venezuela, climbed Miami politics alongside Marco Rubio.

item.title: Trump says U.S. is negotiating end to war with Iran
item.description: Trump said he had told the U.S. military to postpone strikes against Iranian energy infrastructure for five days, while talks proceed.

item.title: Ambulances set ablaze at London synagogue in attack Starmer calls antisemitic
item.description: No one was hurt in the blaze, which damaged four ambulances operated by a Jewish charity in north London’s Golders Green neighborhood, officials said.

item.title: At least one winner emerges from Iran war: U.S. natural gas exporters
item.description: President Donald Trump’s “energy dominance” agenda gets a boost as governments in Asia scramble to substitute Middle East fuel.

item.title: Trump threats, U.S. troop build-up raise specter of battle for Hormuz
item.description: Israelis said securing the strait for energy shipments could become the war’s main goal now that regime change and ending Iran’s ability to obtain a nuclear weapon seem out of reach.

item.title: Joe Kent says he wants Trump to hear MAGA opposition to Iran war
item.description: In an interview with The Post after resigning from the administration in protest, the veteran said he is taking his message to the president’s supporters.

item.title: Iran refuses to give in, seeking greater global economic pain
item.description: Tehran’s unwillingness to capitulate as the war enters its fourth week is wrapped up in the power it exerts over the Strait of Hormuz, officials in the region say.

item.title: When my friend came home from Maduro’s torture prison
item.description: The Washington Post’s Venezuela reporter writes on a couple divided by the authoritarian regime in Caracas — and their joyful reunion.

item.title: IAEA looking into Iran’s report that Natanz nuclear site hit in strikes
item.description: Human rights organizations say they fear Iran will carry out mass executions after the public hangings this week of three men who took place in anti-government protests.

item.title: BTS thrills K-pop fans with free comeback show for thousands in Seoul
item.description: The South Korean megastars thrilled tens of thousands of fans with their first concert in nearly four years following a break for military service and solo careers.

item.title: The Middle East celebrates Eid and Nowruz under the shadow of war
item.description: Missile exchanges have jarred with the spirit of the holidays underway across the region.

item.title: Russian ship potentially bringing oil to Cuba is test of Kremlin allyship
item.description: A Kremlin spokesman said the Russian government is discussing “possible options for assisting Cuba in the difficult situation it finds itself in.”

item.title: To tilt Hungarian election, Russians proposed staging assassination attempt
item.description: To aid Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban, a friend of Russia, in his election, operatives proposed “the Gamechanger” — a staged assassination attempt to stir supporters.

item.title: Trump administration lifts sanctions on millions of barrels of Iranian oil
item.description: The Treasury Department’s authorization of the sale of Iranian crude already at sea is likely to provide revenue for Iran’s war effort against the U.S.

item.title: Cuba rejects U.S. Embassy request to bring in fuel, calls it ‘shameless’
item.description: An effective U.S. oil blockade has worsened the island’s energy crisis. The U.S. Embassy in Havana, too, is feeling the effects.

item.title: 4 women killed in West Bank are first Palestinian deaths in Iran war
item.description: They were at a salon getting their nails done for Eid al-Fitr. It was unclear who fired the missile.

item.title: Hezbollah is bombing northern Israel again. Residents are staying put.
item.description: Rather than evacuation, Israel’s defense minister said the mission now is “to protect northern communities against raids and anti-tank fire.”

item.title: Deepening Iran conflict exposes cracks in U.S. and Israeli objectives
item.description: Israel’s assault on the world’s largest natural gas deposit has renewed tensions between the two allies’ end goals in Iran.

item.title: U.S. mulls lifting oil sanctions on Iran as it hits key Gulf energy sites
item.description: The price of Brent crude briefly climbed past $117 per barrel as Qatar, Saudi Arabia and Kuwait reported more attacks on infrastructure and the U.S. and Iran traded threats.

item.title: Economic fallout from U.S.-led war is hitting the rest of the world harder
item.description: Because of greater exposure to disruptions in the Middle East, such as high natural gas prices, businesses and consumers outside the U.S. are being hit harder by the war in Iran.

item.title: Pakistani strike killed hundreds, Afghanistan says, as regional conflicts boil
item.description: As attention focuses on Iran, the conflict between two of its neighbors is escalating.

item.title: Israel steps up assassinations as Iran war widens to energy facilities
item.description: The conflict continues to roil global energy markets. On Wednesday, an attack on Iran’s South Pars gas field sent energy prices soaring.

item.title: Gabbard tells senators Iranian regime is degraded but still intact
item.description: The director of national intelligence provided the Senate Intelligence Committee with mixed messages about the state of Iran’s nuclear program before the war began.

item.title: Rubio says Cuba’s efforts to improve its economy are failing
item.description: Cuba “can’t fix” its economy, Secretary of State Marco Rubio said, as President Donald Trump said his administration will be “doing something with Cuba very soon.”

item.title: Japan’s leader heads to D.C. bearing gifts of peace for a president at war
item.description: Japan’s super-popular prime minister, Sanae Takaichi, will visit the White House on Thursday, as President Donald Trump looks to allies for military help against Iran.

item.title: This Emirati billionaire put a voice to Gulf anger over Trump’s war in Iran
item.description: In a post on X, the hotel magnate lambasted the president for not considering collateral damage, although he later told The Post: “I blame Trump, but I blame the Iranians more.”

item.title: Hezbollah ignites blowback from its base as over 1 million flee war with Israel
item.description: The militant group’s attacks on Israel have sparked anger even among its most loyal Shiite supporters in Lebanon, weakening its clout as the war widens.

item.title: Israel says it killed Iran’s top security official, along with paramilitary boss
item.description: Ali Larijani, Iran’s lead security official, and Basij commander Gholamreza Soleimani were “eliminated,” Israel said. Iran has not commented on the strikes.

item.title: European leaders rebuff Trump’s call to open the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Trump has expressed frustration over a lack of military assistance from allies, but European leaders are reluctant to join a conflict he started without consulting them.

item.title: U.S. tells all its embassies to ‘immediately’ review security after strikes
item.description: The State Department order to review security at “ALL posts worldwide” follows persistent attacks on U.S. embassies, with almost 300 reported in Iraq alone.

item.title: Senior official openly breaks with White House, resigns over war
item.description: Joe Kent, a close aide to Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard, cited deliberate Israeli “misinformation” and lies to President Donald Trump about a “swift path to victory.”
